title: Esooma

{{Quote|Esoomians are among the best hired muscle in the galaxy, and now that the Imperials are no longer quarantining their planet, they are free to spread out and find work.|[[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" />}}
'''Esooma''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> located in the [[Esooma system/Legends|Esooma system]], within the [[Sprizen sector/Legends|Sprizen sector]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Esoomian/Legends|Esoomian]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who were considered to be among the best [[Mercenary/Legends|hired muscle]] in the galaxy. Esooma was quarantined by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], preventing the Esoomians from leaving their homeworld, up until around<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="7 ABY">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] dates itself to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. As ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], including the Battle of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], it can be assumed that ''Wanted by Cracken'' takes place in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> after which they were free to depart and find work.<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> An Esoomian known as [[Tantor (bounty hunter)|Tantor]] first left Esooma when he was hired as an enforcer for a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]].<ref name="Bounty Hunters">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Esooma was originally created for [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], supplement written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in [[1995]].<ref name="Wanted by Cracken" /> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', [[2009]] a reference book written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], placed the Esooma system, and therefore Esooma itself, in grid square N-5.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
